SIMD-accelerated byte scanning for HTTP parsing.

Uses SSE2 (x86) or scalar fallbacks for CRLF detection. The key trick: left-shifting the \n mask by 1 byte aligns it with \r, allowing us to detect \r\n pairs in a single SIMD pass.

Consts

hasSse2 = true
MaxRequestLine = 8192
Max request line size (must match http.nim)

Procs

func findCRLF(buf: ptr UncheckedArray[byte]; start, maxLen: int): int {.inline,
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Find rn starting from start. Returns index of r, or -1 if not found.

Find rn starting from start. Returns index of r, or -1 if not found. 
func findCRLFSse2(buf: ptr UncheckedArray[byte]; start, limit: int): int {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
SSE2-accelerated rn scanner. 
func findDoubleCRLF(buf: ptr UncheckedArray[byte]; start, maxLen: int): int {.
    inline, ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Find rnrn starting from start. Returns index past the final n, or -1. 
func findDoubleCRLFScalar(buf: ptr UncheckedArray[byte]; start, limit: int): int {.
    inline, ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Find rnrn starting from start. Returns index past the final n, or -1. 
func findDoubleCRLFSse2(buf: ptr UncheckedArray[byte]; start, maxLen: int): int {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

SSE2-accelerated rnrn scanner.

Uses the same shift trick as findCRLFSse2, but applied twice:

  1. Find rn positions (crlfMask = crMask & lfShifted)
  2. Find rnrn positions (crlfMask & crlfShifted-by-2)

This is a single SIMD pass, O(n/16), vs the old sliding-window approach.
